title: centaurea pratensis subsp. debeauxii
intro: Centaurea pratensis subsp. debeauxii, also known as Beaux's knapweed or white knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for wound healing and skin issues. The health benefits of Centaurea pratensis subsp. debeauxii are thought to be due to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help to reduce inflammation and improve overall well-being. Herbal preparations of the plant may be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
